program.name: Rio Tinto Graduate Program (Global)
program.duration_text: Rio Tinto states it is a 2-year graduate program, and also says the development program is completed over 16 to 24 months (depending on location).
program.rotation_text: Rio Tinto describes the graduate development program as being divided into 2 phases, completed over 16 to 24 months (depending on location).

eligibility.work_rights: For graduate roles, you must have the right to work in the country where the position is based (for example: citizens, permanent residents, student or postgraduate work visa holders).
eligibility.graduation_window: For graduate roles, you must have graduated with a relevant university degree and have less than 2 years of relevant work experience.
minimum_requirements: You must have the right to work in the country where the position is based (for example: citizens, permanent residents, student or postgraduate work visa holders).
You must have graduated with a relevant university degree.
You must have less than 2 years of relevant work experience.

tips: Rio Tinto says additional criteria may apply and will be listed in each job advertisement.
Rio Tinto says applications follow the university calendar in each region and only current opportunities are typically listed.
Rio Tinto says if no open roles are listed for your country, you can join their Talent Network to be notified when the next recruitment campaign starts.

exec_themes: Operational excellence (“Best Operator”) with a strong focus on safety, simplification, and accountability.
Cost discipline and improving unit costs over time (including a stated pathway to unit cost improvement through 2030).
Project execution and capital discipline (investing with discipline while protecting balance sheet strength).
Portfolio diversification and growth in “energy transition” materials (notably copper and lithium) alongside iron ore and aluminium.
Maintaining attractive shareholder returns (dividend policy/practice tied to cash generation).
Strengthening “social licence” and partnerships with communities/Indigenous Peoples to support long-term operations.
commercial_context.profit_engine: Rio Tinto’s profit engine is to run large, long-life, low-cost mining and processing assets (especially Pilbara iron ore, plus aluminium/bauxite and copper) with tight cost control and reliable production, generate strong operating cash flow, and then allocate that cash between disciplined reinvestment in growth projects (including copper and lithium), decarbonisation, and dividends to shareholders.
commercial_context.headwinds: Some operations have faced performance challenges (named examples include Iron Ore Company of Canada, Kennecott, and Rio Tinto Iron and Titanium). Results have also been affected by lower iron ore prices (noted as 11% lower in a recent full-year results release), which can reduce earnings and free cash flow even when operations perform well.
commercial_context.esg: Rio Tinto frames ESG around safety, climate, communities/Indigenous Peoples, and responsible environmental management. It reports targets and performance on topics including workplace safety (zero fatalities ambition; AIFR tracking), climate goals (reducing Scope 1 and 2 emissions vs. a 2018 baseline and aiming for net zero operational emissions by 2050), community and human-rights programs (including community perception monitoring and training), land stewardship/rehabilitation and biodiversity work, and tailings management aligned to the Global Industry Standard on Tailings Management (GISTM), with a stated timeline to reach full conformance for remaining facilities by August 2025.
commercial_context.recent_pivot: A notable recent pivot is the accelerated push to diversify toward “energy transition” materials—especially lithium—through the acquisition of Arcadium Lithium (completed 6 March 2025) and the creation of “Rio Tinto Lithium,” alongside continued growth ambitions in copper. Rio Tinto also describes a “stronger, sharper, simpler” way of working and increased commitment to carbon abatement projects as part of its decarbonisation journey.

notes: The global graduate program page describes the program structure, duration framing (2 years; 16–24 months), and lists countries where the graduate program is currently available.
The page does not provide a single global application open or close date; instead it points candidates to current roles by location.
Entry requirements for graduate roles are stated directly on the global graduates and interns page.
The global page gives high-level guidance (apply via current opportunities; additional criteria are in each job ad; join Talent Network if no roles are listed).
Specific recruitment stages (for example: tests, interviews, assessment centres) are not listed on the global page.

Exec themes, profit engine, and recent pivot are directly supported by Rio Tinto’s own strategy and results communications, but some items are phrased in simpler student language and therefore are a plain-English synthesis rather than a single quoted list.

Headwinds are limited to what Rio Tinto explicitly names (asset challenges and iron ore price pressure). It does not provide a complete risk list in the cited pages, so this summary is not exhaustive.

ESG content is grounded in the Sustainability reporting hub, which summarizes 2024 targets/performance and key programs; some figures (eg, fatalities, AIFR, tailings timeline) are explicitly stated there.
